annotations.label: Riemann curvature tensor

anchor.snippet: the most standard one is the curvature tensor, given in terms of a Levi-Civita connection

annotations.note: A grep across Mathlib for curvature/Riemann/Levi-Civita finds no Riemann curvature tensor definition; only basic Riemannian manifold and covariant-derivative scaffolding exist.

annotations.label: Curvature transformation (endomorphism)

anchor.snippet: is also called the curvature transformation or endomorphism

annotations.note: No curvature endomorphism R(u,v) is defined in Mathlib's manifold or vector-bundle files.

annotations.label: Symmetries of the curvature tensor

anchor.snippet: The curvature tensor has the following symmetries

annotations.note: Without a Riemann curvature tensor in Mathlib, its symmetry relations are not formalized.

annotations.label: First Bianchi identity (Ricci)

anchor.snippet: The last identity was discovered by Ricci , but is often called the first Bianchi identity

annotations.note: Grep for 'Bianchi' in Mathlib returns no results.

annotations.label: Completeness of curvature tensor symmetries

anchor.snippet: The three identities form a complete list of symmetries of the curvature tensor

annotations.note: No curvature tensor or its symmetry classification appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Second Bianchi identity

anchor.snippet: The Bianchi identity (often the second Bianchi identity ) involves the covariant derivatives

annotations.note: Grep for 'Bianchi' in Mathlib returns no results.

annotations.label: Sectional curvature

anchor.snippet: Sectional curvature is a further, equivalent but more geometrical, description of the curvature of Riemannian manifolds

annotations.note: Grep for 'sectionalCurvature'/'SectionalCurvature' yields nothing in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sectional curvature formula for two vectors

anchor.snippet: If ⁠   ⁠ are two linearly independent vectors in

annotations.note: The sectional curvature formula is not present since sectional curvature itself is undefined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sectional curvature determines curvature tensor

anchor.snippet: The following formula indicates that sectional curvature describes the curvature tensor completely

annotations.note: Neither sectional curvature nor the Riemann tensor is in Mathlib, so this reconstruction formula is absent.

annotations.label: Curvature form

anchor.snippet: The curvature of an

annotations.note: Grep for 'curvatureForm'/'CurvatureForm' returns no hits.

annotations.label: Connection form

anchor.snippet: Then one can define the connection form, an antisymmetric matrix of 1-forms

annotations.note: Grep for 'connectionForm'/'ConnectionForm' returns no hits; only an unrelated CovariantDerivative scaffold exists.

annotations.label: Curvature form (definition)

anchor.snippet: Then the curvature form ⁠   ⁠ is defined by

annotations.note: No definition of the curvature form Ω = dω + ω∧ω is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First Bianchi identity in curvature-form notation

anchor.snippet: This approach builds in all symmetries of curvature tensor except the first Bianchi identity

annotations.note: Curvature form formalism and Bianchi identities are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Second Bianchi identity in curvature-form notation

anchor.snippet: The second Bianchi identity takes form

annotations.note: Grep for 'Bianchi' in Mathlib returns no results.

annotations.label: Curvature operator

anchor.snippet: It is sometimes convenient to think about curvature as an operator

annotations.note: No curvature operator on Λ²TM is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Scalar curvature

anchor.snippet: Scalar curvature is a function on any Riemannian manifold

annotations.note: Grep for 'scalarCurvature'/'ScalarCurvature' returns no Mathlib hits.

annotations.label: Ricci curvature

anchor.snippet: Ricci curvature is a linear operator on tangent space at a point

annotations.note: Grep for 'ricciCurvature'/'RicciCurvature' yields no Mathlib results.

annotations.label: Weyl curvature tensor

anchor.snippet: The Weyl curvature tensor has the same symmetries as the Riemann curvature tensor, but with one extra constraint

annotations.note: All 'Weyl' hits in Mathlib refer to Weyl groups of root systems, not the Weyl curvature tensor.

annotations.label: Conformal invariance of Weyl tensor

anchor.snippet: The Weyl tensor is invariant with respect to a conformal change of metric

annotations.note: Without the Weyl curvature tensor, its conformal invariance is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vanishing of Weyl tensor in low dimensions and conformal flatness

anchor.snippet: In dimensions 2 and 3 the Weyl tensor vanishes

annotations.note: The Weyl tensor is not defined in Mathlib, so its low-dimensional vanishing and conformal-flatness equivalence are absent.

annotations.label: Ricci decomposition

anchor.snippet: the Riemann curvature tensor can be decomposed into a Weyl part and a Ricci part

annotations.note: None of Riemann/Ricci/Weyl tensors exist in Mathlib, so the decomposition is not formalized.

annotations.label: Conformal rescaling of Riemann tensor

anchor.snippet: it can be used to show that if the metric is rescaled by a conformal factor

annotations.note: Conformal rescaling formulas for the Riemann tensor are not present in Mathlib.
